docs.text:

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
[Replace this paragraph with your own bio. Write 3–4 sentences covering: your background and what you specialize in, the kinds of problems you work on, the tools and methods you use, and what you are currently looking for. Example below:]

I am a geospatial data scientist with a background in remote sensing and machine learning. I work on extracting actionable insights from satellite imagery and large spatial datasets using Python, Google Earth Engine, and open-source GIS tools. I am passionate about applying GeoAI techniques to real-world challenges in land use mapping, climate monitoring, and urban planning. I am currently seeking opportunities in [YOUR TARGET ROLE] in [YOUR TARGET LOCATION].

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• GIS & Remote Sensing

    • QGIS, ArcGIS Pro, Google Earth Engine
    • GDAL / OGR, GRASS GIS
    • Multispectral and SAR image analysis
    • Cloud Native Geospatial (COG, STAC, Zarr)

  • Programming

    • Python — GeoPandas, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib
    • R — sf, terra, ggplot2
    • JavaScript — Leaflet, MapLibre GL
    • SQL, PostgreSQL + PostGIS

  • Machine Learning & GeoAI

    • Supervised classification — Random Forest, XGBoost
    • Deep learning for image segmentation — U-Net, SAM
    • scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow
    • Object detection in satellite imagery

  • Web Mapping & Data

    • Leaflet.js, Folium, MapLibre GL JS
    • Cloud storage — AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats — GeoTIFF, GeoParquet, NetCDF
    • Streamlit for data-driven web apps

  • Data & Cloud

    • PostgreSQL + PostGIS
    • Cloud storage: AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats: GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, NetCDF, Zarr, GeoParquet

  • Drone / UAV Data Processing

    • Mission planning and flight operations
    • Photogrammetry: Agisoft Metashape, OpenDroneMap
    • Point cloud processing: CloudCompare, PDAL

docs.text:
I am open to work opportunities, collaborations, and project discussions. Feel free to reach out through any of the channels below.

Email your-email@example.com GitHub github.com/[YOUR-GITHUB-USERNAME] LinkedIn linkedin.com/in/[YOUR-LINKEDIN-USERNAME] ResearchGate researchgate.net/profile/[YOUR-PROFILE]

Download CV

docs.title: Data Architect / End-to-End Developer — Midwest Biodiversity Institute (Contract)
docs.text:
Apr 2021 – Jan 2025 | Ohio

  • Led migration of a 30+ year-old business-critical database system to a cloud platform, including data, code, input processing, and scientific output.
  • Developed new capabilities in Azure SQL Database and Microsoft Power BI, enabling direct field data entry and analysis on phones and tablets.
  • Converted complex legacy processes to R for use on the new cloud platform.

docs.title: Data Architect / End-to-End Developer — Agrigates Inc. (Contract)
docs.text:
Apr 2021 – Jan 2025 | Ohio

  • Designed and built an integrated analytical system for a startup serving large agricultural enterprises, combining dairy cow activity sensor data, on-farm management systems, and weather data from OpenWeatherMap.
  • Delivered management insights and research-ready data using Azure SQL Database, Azure Analysis Services, and Microsoft Power BI.

docs.title: Data Architect / Team Lead, Data Warehouse — CareSource (Contract)
docs.text:
Apr 2021 – Jan 2025 | Ohio

  • Key technical resource for a newly formed data warehouse development team, building a new instance to provide high-speed access to critical business information.
  • Provided data integration training to team members.
  • Primary platforms: SQL Server, SQL Server Integration Services, and Power BI.

docs.title: Database Developer / Process Automation — Nationwide Insurance (Contract to Hire)
docs.text:
Oct 2018 – Mar 2021 | Ohio

  • Supported the enterprise actuarial department in making best use of a newly implemented large data mart, replacing legacy systems and training business users.
  • Provided performance tuning expertise for very large databases in collaboration with the IT team.

docs.title: Data Architect / Team Lead, Data Warehouse — State of Ohio DODD (Contract)
docs.text:
Aug 2016 – Oct 2018 | Ohio

  • Led a data warehouse team of 9 developers, providing training across the full Business Intelligence stack.
  • Implemented SQL Server Analysis Services for the enterprise and trained DBAs on deployment and security.
  • Enabled interactive access to very large datasets for end users.

docs.title: Business Intelligence Architect — Nationwide Insurance (Contract via TEKsystems)
docs.text:
Jan 2016 – Jul 2016 | Ohio

  • Brought up analytical systems to provide large data warehouse analysis, and trained staff on data modeling.
  • Set up highly tuned models with extracts from a Netezza DataMart.

docs.title: Senior ETL / BI Developer — Various Contracting Firms
docs.text:
Aug 2012 – Jan 2016 | Ohio

  • Delivered data warehouse, ETL, and multidimensional SSAS solutions for clients including American Health Holdings, CareWorksTech, Advanced Computing, Sarnova Inc., and CGI Inc.
  • Responsibilities included SQL Server version migrations, SSIS ETL development, advanced dimension and fact design, MDX reporting, and end-to-end data warehouse builds.
  • Technologies: SSIS, SSRS, SSAS, SQL Server, MDX.

docs.title: Designer / Developer — Cardinal Health (Contract to Hire)
docs.text:
Aug 2003 – Jul 2012 | Ohio

  • Designed and developed management dashboards, inventory analysis systems, and client reporting systems for Enterprise IT Services, Sales Operations, Accounting Services, and Contract Sales and Services divisions.
  • Built a contract and membership processing system using fuzzy-matching algorithms that cut labor by more than half with a 6-month ROI.
  • Developed SharePoint workflow applications to automate document distribution and compensation contract delivery to ~1,000 sales representatives.
  • Technologies: SQL Server Reporting Services, Integration Services, Analysis Services, SharePoint, Nintex Workflow, Visual Studio .NET, T-SQL, ProClarity.

docs.title: Undergraduate Studies in Biology and Chemistry
docs.text: University of Rhode Island & Roger Williams University | Rhode Island

docs.text:

  • Machine Learning with R — University of Cincinnati, 2019
  • Intermediate R — University of Cincinnati, 2019
  • Introduction to Data Science with Python / Building Data Pipelines / Scikit-learn — International Python Conference, 2019
  • Linear Regression, Logistic Regression & the Generalized Linear Model — University of Michigan Summer Session, 2018
  • Bioinformatics: Rigorous Analysis and Design — Ohio State University, 2018
  • R Programming for Biostatistics — University of Michigan Summer Session, 2017
  • SQL Server Predictive Analytics / R Services / R Development — Microsoft Ignite, 2016
  • Molecular Biology Bootcamp — Smith College, 2012
  • Advanced Integration Services / Reporting Services Master Class — Microsoft Tech Ed, 2009
  • SQL Server 2005 / Designing and Implementing OLAP Solutions — Microsoft Tech Ed, 2003–2005
  • VB.Net, ADO.Net, ASP.Net / Designing and Programming SQL Server Databases — Microsoft Tech Ed, 1999–2003
  • Financial Accounting for Managers — Continuing Education

docs.title: Technical Skills
docs.text:
• Databases: SQL Server, Oracle, Netezza, Azure SQL Database, Databricks, Azure Data Warehouse, MS Access
  • BI & Analytics: Power BI, SSAS, SSRS, SSIS, Azure Analysis Services, Tableau, ProClarity, PowerPivot, DAX, MDX
  • Languages: T-SQL, Python, R, VB.Net, ADO.Net, VBA
  • Platforms & Tools: Azure, Visual Studio 2003–2022, SharePoint, Nintex Workflow, ArcGIS Pro, MS Excel

docs.title: Sample Notebook
docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
